## What does this change?

<!-- One or two sentences. If it's a factual correction, say what was wrong. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Factual correction
- [ ] New content (page, section, or expansion)
- [ ] Typo / formatting / broken link
- [ ] Dead video embed replaced
- [ ] Site configuration or tooling

## Sources

<!--
Required for any factual claim, date, figure, or credit. Link it or name it.
"Wikipedia" alone isn't enough — say which article.

Remember that production anecdotes about this film are unusually unreliable.
If accounts conflict, the page should say so rather than pick a winner.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] **No copyrighted text.** No screenplay pages, no passages from the novels, no text copied from another wiki or site. Brief attributed quotation for commentary only.
- [ ] Facts are stated as facts; interpretation is framed as interpretation.
- [ ] Version-specific claims are labeled (*novel*, *1972 film*, *Part II*, *Coda*, *Winegardner*, *game*).
- [ ] Internal links use relative Markdown paths (`../characters/michael-corleone.md`).
- [ ] If I could run it: `mkdocs build --strict` passes. *(CI runs this anyway — a broken internal link will fail the build and block merge.)*

<!--
Don't worry if you can't tick everything. Typo fixes need almost none of this.
Open the PR and we'll sort it out in review.
-->
